items.title: Reflection / Duality
items.meta: Year: 2024  Medium: Oil on canvas  Size: 18 x 24 in
items.description:
In this monochromatic work, I strip away the distraction of color to confront the self in a void of crimson and oxblood. I am attempting to capture the heavy, visceral weight of the gaze. Here, the mirror is not merely an optical tool for verifying appearance, but a psychological threshold where the unified self fractures. By locking eyes with the reflection, I engage in a silent interrogation of identity, exploring the feeling of jamais vu---the unsettling moment when one's own face becomes unrecognizable. The choice of red is deliberate; it evokes a biological interiority, blurring the line between the external space and the blood running beneath the skin. I built up the paint with dense, deliberate strokes to give the atmosphere a suffocating

physicality. Through this piece, I seek to visualize the anxiety of isolation, questioning whether the reflection confirms my existence or reveals the inherent instability of the "I."
